CLAUDE.md — MPCurver Handoff Update

This file is a fast handoff note for Claude. It is intentionally shorter than AGENTS.md and focuses on what changed while the project was being developed in Codex.

If you only have a few minutes, read this first:

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/CLAUDE_5MIN.md

For the exhaustive codebase contract, read:

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/AGENTS.md

1. What Changed Since The Older csmooth_em-Centric Version

The project is no longer centered on csmooth_em.

The current package has:

  1. cavi — the recommended/default single-ordering MPCurve fitter.
  2. csmooth_em / smooth_em — legacy MAP/EM backends retained for benchmarking and backward compatibility.
  3. mpcurve — the unified user-facing wrapper, now defaulting to algorithm = "cavi".
  4. CAVI-based dual-ordering / feature-partition tools in R/10_partition_cavi.R.

In short:

  • old worldview: csmooth_em is the main engine
  • current worldview: cavi is the main engine, csmooth_em is legacy

2. Current Recommended Entry Points

Single ordering

  • fit_mpcurve(..., algorithm = "cavi")
  • do_mpcurve() to continue an mpcurve fit
  • cavi() / do_cavi() when working directly with the raw variational object

Dual ordering / feature partition

  • soft_two_trajectory_cavi()
  • forward_two_ordering_partition_cavi()
  • backward_two_ordering_partition_cavi()

Legacy comparison only

  • fit_mpcurve(..., algorithm = "csmooth_em")
  • soft_two_trajectory_EM()
  • forward_two_ordering_partition_csmooth()
  • backward_two_ordering_partition_csmooth()

3. Current Documentation Map

If you want to get re-oriented quickly, read these in order:

  1. /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/mpcurve_intro.Rmd Current single-ordering introduction.
  2. /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/partition.Rmd Current dual-ordering introduction.
  3. /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/cavi_math_details.Rmd Paper-style derivation of the CAVI updates.
  4. /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/cavi_vs_csmooth_math.Rmd Conceptual and mathematical comparison of cavi and csmooth_em.
  5. /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/elife_fitness_analysis.Rmd Real-data workflow on the eLife mutant-fitness dataset.

Legacy / historical docs:

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/csmooth_em_intro.Rmd
  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/partition_csmooth.Rmd
  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/Intro.Rmd

Important:

  • Intro.Rmd is now historical / archival. Do not treat it as the current introduction to the package.

4. Current Class Architecture

There are now four S3 classes:

  • cavi
  • csmooth_em
  • smooth_em
  • mpcurve

mpcurve is the user-facing wrapper.

cavi is the actively developed main algorithm.

5. High-Level Status Of The Main Algorithms

cavi single-ordering path

This path is now in good shape.

What has been checked repeatedly:

  • the objective of interest is elbo_trace
  • the E-step analogue includes the uncertainty correction from diag(S_j)
  • single-ordering sanity checks on model-matched simulations behave well
  • spiral / swiss-roll style examples work well with suitable initialization
  • fit_mpcurve() now defaults to this path

Important interpretation:

  • loglik_trace on cavi fits is a plug-in observed log-likelihood diagnostic, not the exact marginal log-likelihood of the latent-U model and not the monotonicity certificate

Legacy csmooth_em path

Still useful for:

  • benchmark comparisons
  • backward compatibility
  • historical method comparisons

But it is no longer the conceptual center of the package.

6. Dual-Ordering / Soft Partition Status

The newer cavi partition path is implemented in:

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/R/10_partition_cavi.R

Main message:

  • the cavi soft-partition route has a cleaner objective story than the older soft_two_trajectory_EM() route
  • however, non-monotone feature sets remain initialization-sensitive
  • partition recovery can be good even when recovery of the specific latent ordering / trajectory is weaker

This is not automatically an objective-bookkeeping bug.

A key empirical lesson from recent work:

  • when trajectories are monotone, partitioning and ordering recovery are often both good
  • when many features are non-monotone, the harder problem is often initialization / basin selection, not basic CAVI correctness

7. Initialization Lessons Learned

Single-ordering cavi

  • PCA can be fine in easy regimes
  • isomap can be substantially better in harder non-monotone regimes
  • fiedler is useful, but performance depends strongly on geometry

Fiedler fix

fiedler_ordering() was updated:

  • if the user leaves k at the default and the kNN graph is disconnected, the function now automatically increases k until the graph becomes connected
  • if the user explicitly sets k, that explicit choice is preserved

So if you see older notes complaining that default Fiedler drops most cells through graph fragmentation, that is now outdated.

8. Real-Data Example Added

There is now a paper-style real-data vignette based on:

  • inst/extdata/elife-61271-fig2-data1-v2.csv

See:

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/vignettes/elife_fitness_analysis.Rmd

This dataset is a mutant-by-environment fitness matrix:

  • rows = mutants
  • columns = environments
  • entries = fitness scores

The vignette explores:

  • single-ordering cavi
  • dual-ordering soft partition
  • initialization sensitivity
  • partition composition by environment
  • estimated latent-position weights pi

There is also a separate HTML note for additional scatterplot diagnostics in:

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/output/html/elife_partition_scatter_note.Rmd

9. Dependencies And Metadata Were Updated

DESCRIPTION now needs to reflect the fact that the code uses packages such as:

  • Matrix
  • matrixStats
  • mclust
  • parallel
  • MASS
  • RANN
  • RSpectra
  • Rtsne
  • igraph
  • princurve

If you notice a mismatch between code and declared dependencies, trust the code first and update DESCRIPTION.

10. Files That Matter Most Right Now

  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/R/09_cavi.R Main single-ordering variational implementation.
  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/R/10_partition_cavi.R Main dual-ordering CAVI implementation.
  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/R/07_mpcurve.R Unified user-facing API.
  • /Users/ziangzhang/Desktop/MPCurver/AGENTS.md Full project contract / implementation guidance.

11. Do Not Regress These Points

  1. Do not re-center the package narrative around csmooth_em.
  2. Do not describe cavi as a prototype anymore.
  3. Do not treat cavi loglik_trace as the true marginal log-likelihood.
  4. Do not remove the uncertainty correction from the cavi responsibility step.
  5. Do not describe legacy partition tools as the recommended user workflow.
  6. Do not forget that Intro.Rmd is historical, not current.

12. Suggested Mental Model

The package is now organized around this split:

  • Theory / principled default: cavi
  • Legacy benchmark / historical comparison: csmooth_em
  • Unified public API: mpcurve

If you start from that picture, the rest of the current codebase will make much more sense than if you start from the older EM-only picture.

On this page
